title: thymus longicaulis var. subisophyllus
intro: Thymus longicaulis var. subisophyllus, also known as wild marjoram, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of wild marjoram include relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Wild marjoram can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in cooking and natural remedies due to its flavorful and medicinal properties.
